chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Constitutional Petition No. D-1095 of 2026 (Ashraf Masih versus Province of Sindh & others) Date Order with signature of Judge Mr. Justice Adnan-ul-Karim Memon Mr. Justice Zulfiqar Ali Sangi 1. For order on office objections 2. For hearing of main case Date of hearing and order: 20.4.2026 Mr. Rustam Khan Mahar advocate for the petitioner Mr. Asad Ahmed advocate for the respondent / KMC --------------------- ORDER Adnan-ul-Karim Memon, J. Petitioner Ashraf Masih has filed this Constitutional Petition under Article 199 of the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973, seeking following relief: - a. To declare that the petitioner is legally entitled for recovery of retirements benefits Gratuity etc. from the respondents and the respondents are bound to act under the framework provided in constitution of Pakistan and to obey the law being the statutory body incorporated under the provincial government. b. To direct the respondents to release retirement benefits etc. forthwith without committing any further delay as the respondents being statutory body is bound to act in accordance with law and recovery of dues is the fundamental right of the petitioner and respondent is bound under principle policy to do so. It is, inter alia, contended that the petitioner was appointed as Sanitary Worker (BPS-02) on 20.2.1990 and retired from service on 19.2.2025, since then his pensionary benefits have not been released. Learned counsel, representing the KMC submits that his office has already received the pension papers of the petitioner for finalization of Rs.1,651,802/-, however, he is drawing monthly pension from the HBL Branch regularly.

It is, inter alia, contended that the petitioner was appointed as Sanitary Worker (BPS-02) on 20.2.1990 and retired from service on 19.2.2025, since then his pensionary benefits have not been released. Learned counsel, representing the KMC submits that his office has already received the pension papers of the petitioner for finalization of Rs.1,651,802/-, however, he is drawing monthly pension from the HBL Branch regularly. So far as the remaining pension commutation of Rs.1,651,802/- is concerned, the same shall be paid within three weeks’ time. In view of the above position, learned counsel for the petitioner seeks disposal of this petition in terms of the statement so made by learned counsel for the KMC. Petition stands disposed of. JUDGE JUDGE Zahid/*
